Scenario.title: Honeybee Attack on Transportation System
Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Simple A sophisticated spearphishing campaign targeting transportation sector employees leads to unauthorized access, exploitation of vulnerable applications, and manipulation of critical traffic control systems.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Application Log, OS Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Application Log Content (Mechanism), Data Manipulation

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker crafts highly targeted phishing emails mimicking trusted sources, embedding malicious links that compromise employee credentials when clicked. This leverages the internet-facing assets surface through social engineering vectors to establish initial foothold.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Using stolen credentials, the attacker exploits unpatched vulnerabilities in public-facing transportation application logs. By manipulating log content, they execute arbitrary code remotely, escalating privileges within the system architecture.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker alters real-time traffic control OS processes by injecting malformed data payloads. This disrupts operational systems, creating safety hazards and service outages through controlled data corruption mechanisms.

Scenario.summary: A multi-stage attack compromising transportation systems through phishing, application exploitation, and data manipulation demonstrates advanced persistence capabilities targeting critical infrastructure.
